guide_review_response.verdict: ITEM_2_7_V2_VERIFIED_CLEAN -- V matrix work is excellent; AUTHORIZE 3-line docstring fix in server.py per dn19 (NOT rule 5b(a) as EA-6 framed); PROCEED to Item 2.8
guide_review_response.work_item: 2.7.v2 V matrix V4-V7 NEW tests verification + server.py D4 docstring drift disposition

reviewing.ea_artifact: EA-6 chat report at Item 2.7.v2 closure with discovery-surfaced docstring drift

guide_review_response.summary:
Item 2.7.v2 work is comprehensive and demonstrates mature engineering across all 4 V matrix tests. Independent disk verification confirms every claim: 4 NEW test files at tools/tests/ (V4 12 tests, V5 1 test, V6 50 tests, V7 6 tests = 69 NET-ADD; pytest baseline 282 → 351 verified by arithmetic); each test file is well-designed and appropriately scoped; standing invariants intact (HEAD f863a22 unchanged; working tree 33 entries -- the 4 NEW tests inside the existing tools/tests/ ?? directory don't increase the count; stash byte-identical; state.json verbatim).
EA-6's design choices are all sharp: - V4: correctly scoped to the 6 D2-completion targets (the actual subset of refactored Python files with argparse main() exposing --project-root + --project-id). Subprocess.run + --help introspection over each entrypoint; returncode + flag-presence assertions resilient to argparse output behavior. - V5: simplest path per Guide-#6 g2 -- subprocess.run to existing verify_25_v2.ps1 + assert ALL_GREEN trailer. Re-uses the proven 20/20 PASS probe end-to-end without re-implementing AST parsing in Python. Mature skip-if-non-Windows + skip-if-pwsh-missing handling. - V6: elegant simplification BEYOND the init pointer's "mock-based" suggestion. EA-6 recognized that _require_project_id raises BEFORE any HTTP call, so direct handler invocation (no HTTP mocking needed) is sufficient to verify the rejection chain. 7 GATED_HANDLERS × 7 BAD_PROJECT_IDS = 49 parametrized tests + 1 self-discovering audit test (uses inspect.getsource() to count actual _require_project_id invocations and assert match with GATED_HANDLERS list). 50/69 = great test density on the most-defended axis. - V7: multi-axis byte-identity engineering -- 4 distinct tests covering (1) distinct identity per project, (2) structural-field-shape equality, (3) diff-isolates-to-identity-fields-only (catches cross-project state leakage), (4) round-trip per synthetic project (parametrized × 3: alpha/beta/gamma). Pragmatic structural-identity framing (not literal byte-identity) -- semantically the right invariant to test for project portability.
DISCOVERY-SURFACED FINDING (EA-6 caught the D4 docstring drift): server.py module docstring at lines 22 + 53 says "all 8 _require_project_id call sites" but actual grep finds EXACTLY 7 (lines 229, 249, 271, 308, 359, 668, 700). The docstring at line 22 enumerates 7 named handlers + "and the additional project-scoped handler" (the phantom 8th). The "additional" reference doesn't correspond to any real handler; the count is off by one. project_summary() at line 723 takes no project_id and iterates all projects via /projects, so it's correctly NOT a gated handler. EA-6's 7-count reconciliation is correct.
DISPOSITION DIVERGENCE FROM EA-6's PROPOSAL: EA-6 framed the docstring drift as "STATUS-staleness rule 5b(a)" with documentary disposition in 2.10 STATUS append. Guide-#6 OVERRIDES that framing with a less conservative disposition: this is NOT rule 5b(a) (which targets EXTERNAL state -- git status, scheduled tasks, fleet pause), it's an INTERNAL code-doc defect inside a file we're committing at 2.9. Per dn19 ("Do NOT defer in-scope defect closures to a different stage when the fix is trivial"), the right disposition is FIX THE DOCSTRING in-place. The fix is a trivial 3-line edit: - Line 22: "Decision: KEEP all 8" → "Decision: KEEP all 7" - Line 24-25: remove ", and the additional project-scoped handler" (phantom 8th) - Line 53: "all 8 calls RETAINED" → "all 7 calls RETAINED" EA-6's V6 self-discovering audit test stays as defensive enforcement (catches future drift if a maintainer adds an 8th handler without updating the test fixture). Both pieces -- source-fix + audit-test -- are in-scope per Stage 2 single-commit pattern; the audit test was already authored at 2.7.v2; the source fix can be appended as the FIRST action of the next turn (before 2.8 BLARAI_PID gate).
NEW ACK g6-ea6_n9 captures both the source fix authorization + the audit test enforcement for the cumulative 2.10 STATUS append (footnote set now 12 items).
The Stage 2 foundation is solid. EA-6 has consistently delivered "mature not minimal" work: every V matrix test is well-designed; every disposition decision is surfaced cleanly; every invariant preserved; every audit-chain element forward-staged for 2.10. EA-6 may proceed to Item 2.8 ($BLARAI_PID==3 hard gate) after the docstring fix lands.

independent_disk_verification.check: git log --oneline -1: f863a22 (HEAD) -- ZERO new commits since v5 emission. git status --porcelain | wc -l: 33 entries (22 M + 2 D + 9 ??) -- structurally unchanged (the tools/tests/ directory is a single ?? entry; adding 4 files inside doesn't increase the count). stash@{0}: byte-identical sdo 20260424_013002 -- 6-cycle preservation (v3 → v4 → Guide-5/6 → v5 → Item 2.7 → Item 2.7.v2). state.json: fleet_paused=true; reason "platform separation v2 in progress"; last_updated_by="copilot_agent"; last_updated_utc="2026-04-24T20:46:12Z" -- preserved.

ls tools/tests/ confirms (sizes from filesystem): __init__.py (0 bytes) test_project_context.py (3340 bytes; Stage 2.2 deliverable) test_v_matrix_v4_cli_flag.py (2956 bytes; NEW) test_v_matrix_v5_ps1_blarairoot.py (2230 bytes; NEW) test_v_matrix_v6_mcp_project_id.py (4133 bytes; NEW) test_v_matrix_v7_cross_project_byte_identity.py (5027 bytes; NEW) test_vikunja_client_scope.py (2684 bytes; Stage 2.1 + f1 fix) __pycache__/ 6 test files post-2.7.v2 (was 2 pre-2.7.v2). Authoring date 2026-04-26 20:54 for all 4 NEW files (consistent with this session). Total tests/ directory size growth acceptable.

grep -n '_require_project_id' tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py: Line 22: Decision: KEEP all 8 ... call sites at the ← DOCSTRING-CLAIM Line 31: ``_require_project_id`` is exported from ... ← docstring narrative Line 71: from tools._vikunja_client import _require_project_id ← import Line 229: _require_project_id(project_id) ← INVOCATION 1 Line 249: _require_project_id(project_id) ← INVOCATION 2 Line 271: _require_project_id(project_id) ← INVOCATION 3 Line 308: _require_project_id(project_id) ← INVOCATION 4 Line 359: _require_project_id(project_id) ← INVOCATION 5 Line 668: _require_project_id(project_id) ← INVOCATION 6 Line 700: _require_project_id(project_id) ← INVOCATION 7 Total invocations: 7 (NOT 8 as docstring lines 22 + 53 claim). The docstring at line 22 enumerates 7 named handlers (get_project, update_project, delete_project, list_tasks, create_task, search_tasks, bulk_create_tasks) plus "and the additional project-scoped handler" (the phantom 8th). The "additional" reference does NOT correspond to any real handler. EA-6's reconciliation that project_summary() at line 723 is non-gated (iterates /projects, no project_id) is correct -- it's correctly NOT a gated handler.
DISPOSITION: docstring needs 3-line fix per dn19 (in-scope defect closure; NOT rule 5b(a) STATUS-staleness as EA-6 framed). See <disposition_for_docstring_fix> below.

Read test_v_matrix_v4_cli_flag.py: - REFACTORED_ENTRYPOINTS list (6 modules): exactly the 6 D2-completion targets (daily_digest, welcome_back_digest, credential_rotation_reminder, weekly_summary, dashboard_maintainer, gate_stale_cleaner.run_live). - 2 parametrized tests: test_entrypoint_exposes_project_root_flag + test_entrypoint_exposes_project_id_flag. - 6 entrypoints × 2 tests = 12 tests ✓. - subprocess.run with [sys.executable, "-m", module, "--help"] (correct invocation). - Assertions: returncode == 0 + flag string present in stdout+stderr (resilient to argparse output behavior). Design quality: clean. Sharp scoping to the 6 D2-completion targets (NOT all 13 refactored Python files; 7 of those don't have main() with argparse, so they correctly fall outside V4 scope).

Read test_v_matrix_v5_ps1_blarairoot.py: - 1 test: test_verify_25_v2_probe_emits_all_green. - skip-if non-Windows ✓ (skipif sys.platform != "win32"; Windows-only PS1). - skip-if neither pwsh nor powershell on PATH ✓ (pytest.skip with shutil.which). - subprocess.run pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/verify_25_v2.ps1. - Assertions: returncode == 0 + "ALL_GREEN" trailer in output. Design quality: simplest viable approach per Guide-#6 g2 strategy. Re-uses the proven 20/20 PASS probe end-to-end without Python-side AST re-implementation. Mature cross-platform handling.

Read test_v_matrix_v6_mcp_project_id.py: - GATED_HANDLERS list (7 handlers): get_project, update_project, delete_project, list_tasks, create_task, search_tasks, bulk_create_tasks. Comment at line 31 explicitly notes "project_summary is NOT included: it iterates ALL projects via /projects and does not gate on a single project_id" -- correct reconciliation. - BAD_PROJECT_IDS list (7 values): None, "1" (string), True, False, 3.0 (float), 0, -1. Covers all chokepoint contract rejection cases per f1 fix (None/bool/non-int/<=0). - parametrized: 7 handlers × 7 bad values = 49 tests (test_handler_rejects_bad_project_id). - + 1 audit test (test_gated_handlers_set_matches_require_project_id_call_sites) at line 80-99: uses inspect.getsource(server) to count `_require_project_id(project_id)` invocation lines and assert match with len(GATED_HANDLERS). - Total: 50 tests ✓. Design quality: SUPERIOR TO INIT POINTER'S MOCK-BASED SUGGESTION. EA-6 recognized that _require_project_id raises BEFORE any HTTP call, so direct handler invocation is sufficient (no HTTP mocking needed; no test-double scaffolding). The _invoke_handler helper at lines 46-69 supplies minimum required other args per handler (e.g., title="x" for create_task; query="x" for search_tasks). Sharp simplification.
The audit test is particularly notable: it's self-discovering (counts actual code invocations rather than maintaining a separate ground-truth fixture). If a future maintainer adds an 8th gated handler in server.py without adding it to GATED_HANDLERS in this test file, the audit test fires with a clear error message. This is exactly the defensive engineering pattern that catches downstream drift.

Read test_v_matrix_v7_cross_project_byte_identity.py: - _materialize_synthetic_project helper: creates minimal registry file (just "_project_id: ") in tmp_path subtree. - 3 distinct tests + 1 parametrized test = 6 total ✓: * test_two_synthetic_projects_resolve_to_distinct_contexts (alpha + beta) * test_cross_project_structural_shape_byte_identical (dataclass field set equality across alpha + beta) * test_cross_project_diff_isolates_to_identity_fields_only (assert that any field difference between alpha + beta belongs to {root, name, vikunja_project_id} -- catches cross-project state leakage) * test_resolve_round_trip_per_synthetic_project (parametrized × 3: alpha, beta, gamma) -- 3 tests Design quality: pragmatic structural-identity framing rather than literal byte-identity. The "diff isolates to identity fields only" test (test_cross_project_diff_isolates_to_identity_fields_only) is sharp defensive engineering -- catches accidental cross-project state leakage (e.g., a global-cached field that fails to refresh between resolves). Multi-axis approach verifies project portability at type-level + value-level + isolation-level.

git status --porcelain shows the same 22 M + 2 D + 9 ?? structure as pre-2.7.v2. The 4 NEW test files are inside tools/tests/ which was already a ?? directory entry; no new top-level paths added. server.py + the 6 D2-completion targets + chokepoint + project_context untouched. Stage 2 invariants strictly preserved.

disposition_for_docstring_fix.ea_6_proposed_disposition: EA-6 framed the docstring drift as "STATUS-staleness rule 5b(a)" with documentary disposition in 2.10 STATUS append. EA-6's intent: leave the docstring as-is (under no-amend doctrine reasoning); rely on the V6 audit test for runtime enforcement; document the discrepancy in audit narrative.
disposition_for_docstring_fix.guide_6_disposition: OVERRIDE -- AUTHORIZE source-level fix per dn19, NOT rule 5b(a)
disposition_for_docstring_fix.rationale:
Three independent considerations support FIX-IN-SOURCE over DOCUMENT-IN-2.10:
(1) RULE 5b(a) APPLIES TO EXTERNAL STATE, NOT INTERNAL CODE-DOC DEFECTS. Guide-#6 operating rule 5b(a) explicit text: "STATUS staleness -- repo state is internally consistent but STATUS hasn't caught up. Symptom: STATUS references a branch that's been merged and deleted, or an older HEAD commit, or a stale fleet pause reason." Examples in the rule: branch state, HEAD commit, fleet pause reason, scheduled task counts, vikunja process counts (rule 5b(a) IS appropriate for V4=1 drift). The rule was authored for EXTERNAL state -- things STATUS.md tracks but the procedure can't actively enforce. The server.py D4 docstring is INTERNAL code documentation inside a Python source file. It is NOT external state; it IS code-internal authoritative metadata. Rule 5b(a) doesn't apply.
(2) NO-AMEND DOCTRINE DOES NOT APPLY TO UNCOMMITTED M-STATE FILES. The no-amend doctrine applies to COMMITTED audit artifacts -- v4 mid-stage handoff XML, v5 mid-stage handoff XML, prior Guide review files. server.py is in M state in the working tree; it has NOT been committed since the v5 commit (f863a22) only contained the v5 handoff XML, not server.py. The 2.9 single Stage-2 commit will fold server.py + all other M files. Modifying server.py before 2.9 is NORMAL Stage 2 work, not an amendment to a committed artifact.
(3) DEFECT CLOSURE INSIDE IN-SCOPE WORK PER dn19 + dn20. dn19: "Do NOT defer in-scope defect closures to a different stage when the fix is trivial." The 3-line docstring fix is trivial; server.py is in-scope (M; will be committed at 2.9); leaving the defect for "Stage 6 hardening" or "documentary disposition only" violates dn19. dn20: "Do NOT skip the 'mature not minimal' disk-verification step on EA reports that look clean." The discipline cuts both ways -- when disk verification surfaces a defect (here: docstring vs grep mismatch), the mature response is fix-in-source, not document-in-narrative.
EA-6's V6 audit test stays as DEFENSIVE FORWARD ENFORCEMENT (catches future drift if a maintainer adds an 8th handler without updating the test fixture). The source fix is the COMPLEMENT, not a SUBSTITUTE: the audit test enforces the invariant going forward; the source fix corrects the current state. Both are needed for a clean audit chain.

required_3_line_fix.edit: BEFORE: Decision: KEEP all 8 ``_require_project_id(project_id)`` call sites at the AFTER: Decision: KEEP all 7 ``_require_project_id(project_id)`` call sites at the

BEFORE: ``create_task``, ``search_tasks``, ``bulk_create_tasks``, and the additional project-scoped handler. This DIVERGES from Guide-#6's chokepoint-only (B) AFTER: ``create_task``, ``search_tasks``, and ``bulk_create_tasks``. This DIVERGES from Guide-#6's chokepoint-only (B)
BEFORE: Disposition of guards: all 8 calls RETAINED, none added, none removed. No AFTER: Disposition of guards: all 7 calls RETAINED, none added, none removed. No
required_3_line_fix.also_consider_optional: OPTIONAL but recommended: add a 1-line note to the docstring at the end of the Decision block clarifying the project_summary exclusion, e.g., near line 26-27: "(Note: project_summary handler iterates /projects via no-project_id signature and is correctly NOT a gated handler.)" This pre-empts future maintainer confusion about why project_summary lacks a guard. EA-6 can include this clarification at their discretion.

disposition_for_docstring_fix.when_to_apply: As the FIRST substantive action of the next chat turn, BEFORE proceeding to Item 2.8 BLARAI_PID hard gate. The fix is fast (~30 seconds via Edit tool); doing it now keeps Item 2.7.v2's audit chain clean (each item closes with its source state correct) rather than carrying the docstring defect into 2.8/2.9 narrative. Verification: re-run pytest tools/ after the fix; expect 351 passed (the docstring change has no runtime effect; V6 audit test continues to pass since the GATED_HANDLERS list is still 7 elements).

new_acknowledgment.ack:
Server.py D4 docstring drift disposition (Option-Fix-Source authorized by Guide-#6 per dn19; OVERRIDES EA-6's proposed rule-5b(a) framing).
EA-6 acknowledgment required at start of next chat turn (combine with 2.8 BLARAI_PID gate work): (a) Apply the 3-line docstring fix to tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py per <required_3_line_fix> above (lines 22, 24-25, 53). Optionally add project_summary clarification note. (b) Re-run pytest tools/ -- expect 351 passed (no runtime impact from docstring change; V6 audit test continues to pass). (c) Document both the source-fix AND the V6 audit test in the 2.10 STATUS append "Discovery-surfaced findings during 2.7.v2 V matrix authoring" subsection. Required text (verbatim) for 2.10:
"Stage 2.7.v2 V matrix V6 authoring surfaced a docstring-vs-code discrepancy in tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py: the D4 decision block (lines 22 + 53) said 'all 8 _require_project_id call sites' but the actual code contains EXACTLY 7 invocations (lines 229, 249, 271, 308, 359, 668, 700). The 7 named handlers in the docstring matched the 7 actual sites; the 'and the additional project-scoped handler' phrase was a phantom 8th reference (project_summary correctly does NOT gate on project_id since it iterates /projects). Per Guide-#6 dn19 ('Do NOT defer in-scope defect closures'), the docstring was fixed in-place at Item 2.8 prep (3-line edit: '8'→'7' at lines 22+53; remove ', and the additional project-scoped handler' at lines 24-25). The V6 audit test (test_gated_handlers_set_matches_require_project_id_call_sites) uses inspect.getsource() to count actual `_require_project_id(project_id)` invocations and asserts match with the GATED_HANDLERS list -- providing defensive forward enforcement against future drift. Disposition source: Guide-#6 review file docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_ITEM_2_7_V2_VERIFIED_DOCSTRING_FIX_AUTHORIZED.xml."
Status transitions to SATISFIED at 2.10 STATUS append landing. ack g6-ea6_n9 joins the cumulative 2.10 footnote set (now 12 items): A11 + g5-7/8/9/10 + f1 + encoding regression + 2.5.v2 evidence + v4 D2 footnote + v5 C1-C4 footnote + Option A architectural decision (D2-completion hoist) + g5-4 SATISFIED + g6-ea6_n7 D1 NO-ACTION dispositions + g6-ea6_n8 V4 disposition + g6-ea6_n9 D4 docstring fix.

commendation.topic: V6 elegant simplification beyond init pointer mock-based suggestion

commendations_for_ea_6.commendation: The init pointer + Guide-#6 g3 guidance suggested mock-based testing for V6. EA-6 recognized that the chokepoint helper _require_project_id raises ProjectScopeError BEFORE any HTTP call is issued, so direct handler invocation (without HTTP mocking) is sufficient to verify the rejection chain. This is a SHARPER design than the mock-based variant: less test scaffolding, faster execution, more direct semantic coverage, and the test failures (when they fire) point directly at the chokepoint contract rather than at a mocking layer. Plus the self-discovering audit test (test_gated_handlers_set_matches_require_project_id_call_sites) uses inspect.getsource() to count actual code invocations -- defensive forward enforcement that catches future maintainer drift. Two design wins on a single test file.

commendation.topic: V4 sharp scoping to 6 D2-completion targets

commendations_for_ea_6.commendation: EA-6 correctly identified that V4 ("CLI flag exposure parametrized per refactored entrypoint") should test the SUBSET of refactored Python files that actually have argparse main() exposing --project-root + --project-id flags. Of the 13 refactored files (12 Python + server.py), only 6 have argparse main(); the others are library modules (self_check.py, blarai_next_task_resolver.py, _vikunja_client.py legacy, cleaner.py, diff_builder.py, active_tasks.py) or use startup-ctx-resolution (server.py file 13 D4 pattern). EA-6's REFACTORED_ENTRYPOINTS list of 6 maps 1-to-1 with the D2-completion targets -- elegant scoping discipline.

V7 implements 4 distinct test functions covering structural shape (dataclass fields identical across projects), value diff isolation (only {root, name, vikunja_project_id} differ), distinct identity (alpha vs beta produce distinct contexts), and round-trip robustness (parametrized × 3 synthetic projects: alpha/beta/gamma). The test_cross_project_diff_isolates_to_identity_fields_only test is particularly sharp -- it catches accidental cross-project state leakage (e.g., a global-cached field that fails to refresh between resolves), which is a subtle failure mode that simpler "are the projects different" tests would miss. Pragmatic structural-identity framing rather than literal byte-identity is the right semantic interpretation for project-portability claims.

commendation.topic: V5 simplest path with mature cross-platform handling

commendations_for_ea_6.commendation: EA-6 chose Guide-#6 g2 simplest path (subprocess.run to verify_25_v2.ps1) but added MATURE cross-platform handling: skip-if-non-Windows (the probe is PS1; PowerShell runtime needed) + skip-if-pwsh-or-powershell-missing (defensive against environments without PowerShell on PATH). Re-uses the proven 20/20 PASS probe end-to-end without Python-side AST re-implementation. 1-test-as-integration-of-20-axes is the right density.

The docstring-vs-grep discrepancy (lines 22+53 say "all 8" but actual is 7) was NOT blocking V6's pass-rate (the audit test asserts equality with GATED_HANDLERS, not with the docstring claim). EA-6 surfaced the discrepancy proactively as part of the V6 implementation narrative + proposed a disposition rather than silently ignoring it. Even though Guide-#6 OVERRIDES EA-6's proposed rule-5b(a) framing in favor of a source-level fix, the act of surfacing-with-disposition is the mature discipline. The defect would have shipped silently into the 2.9 single Stage-2 commit if EA-6 had not surfaced it.

commendation.topic: Stage 2 invariants preserved through 4-file authoring + V8 imports + V matrix verification

commendations_for_ea_6.commendation: Read-only verification work (V8 imports + spot-check Python invocations) + 4 NEW test file authoring (purely additive); HEAD f863a22 unchanged; stash byte-identical (6-cycle preservation); state.json verbatim; no governance edits; no merge to main; no fleet cycle. Working tree count unchanged structurally (the 4 NEW test files are inside the existing tools/tests/ ?? directory entry). Discipline maintained through a substantial authoring cycle.

required_acknowledgments.ack_to_emit_at_start_of_next_chat_turn: Per g6-ea6_n9 above: EA-6 acknowledges the Guide-#6 OVERRIDE on docstring-fix framing (dn19, NOT rule 5b(a)) and applies the 3-line fix to tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py as the FIRST action of the next chat turn (before 2.8 BLARAI_PID gate work). pytest re-run after fix expected to show 351 passed (no runtime impact). Both the source-fix AND the V6 audit test will be documented in the 2.10 STATUS append per the verbatim footnote text in g6-ea6_n9.

foundation_solidity_check_per_la_motto.intro: The LA's recurring concern: "is this work risky / are we building on a solid foundation?" The motto is "mature not minimal." Foundation check at Item 2.7.v2 close (per LA framing):
foundation_solidity_check_per_la_motto.evidence: - pytest 282 → 351 passed (+69 NET-ADD; zero regressions). Stage 2 test coverage substantially expanded. - V matrix V4-V7 NEW tests cover the 4 axes from the original Stage 2.6.v2 spec (CLI flag exposure; PS1 -BlarAIRoot; MCP project_id rejection; cross-project byte-identity). - V6's audit test (self-discovering inspect.getsource() count check) is defensive against future drift. - V7's diff-isolation test catches cross-project state leakage. - The docstring drift caught and disposed (source-fix authorized; audit test as forward enforcement). - All Stage 2 invariants preserved through Items 2.6 + 2.7 + 2.7.v2 cycle: HEAD unchanged; stash byte-identical (6-cycle); state.json verbatim; no governance edits; no merge; fleet stays paused. - Cumulative 2.10 footnote set is 12 items deep, all forward-staged with verbatim text in disposition files. No 2.10-omission hazards. - D2 + D3 + D4 + D2-completion + D1 + V matrix V4-V7 all closed with explicit Guide-#6 disposition or APPROVED state. - g5-4 SATISFIED (D2-completion); g6-ea6_n1/n5 SATISFIED (D2-completion path); g6-ea6_n7 staged at 2.10 (D1 NO-ACTION); g6-ea6_n8 staged at 2.10 (V4 disposition); g6-ea6_n9 staged at 2.10 (D4 docstring fix).
foundation_solidity_check_per_la_motto.verdict: The Stage 2 foundation is SOLID. Each disposition decision has been surfaced cleanly, verified independently by Guide-#6 disk inspection, and forward-staged for the 2.10 audit narrative. EA-6's "mature not minimal" track record across 4 cycles (iter-1 gate + cfg-resolution + D1 grep + V4 vikunja drift + V matrix authoring + docstring drift) is consistent: thoroughness over speed; STOP-and-ask at every disposition fork; verbatim restatement; forward-staging audit chain. Remaining work (2.8 + 2.9 + 2.10) is small-scope: 2.8 is a single python -c hard gate; 2.9 is the single Stage-2 commit (high-stakes but mechanical); 2.10 is append-only STATUS. EA-6 is well-positioned to complete Stage 2 in this session pending the docstring fix + remaining items.

guide_review_response.proceed_authorization:
PROCEED -- in the next chat turn, EA-6 executes:
Step 1: Apply the 3-line docstring fix to tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py per <required_3_line_fix> (line 22: "all 8" → "all 7"; line 24-25: remove ", and the additional project-scoped handler"; line 53: "all 8" → "all 7"). Optional: add 1-line project_summary clarification note. Step 2: Re-run pytest tools/ --tb=short -q -- expect 351 passed (no runtime impact; V6 audit test continues to pass). Step 3: Execute Item 2.8 ($BLARAI_PID==3 hard gate per g4-5): python -c "from tools import _project_context; ctx = _project_context.resolve(); assert ctx.vikunja_project_id == 3, f'expected 3, got {ctx.vikunja_project_id}'; print('OK ctx.vikunja_project_id =', ctx.vikunja_project_id)" ON DRIFT: STOP. Do NOT proceed to commit. Report to LA per g4-5. NO silent regex/yaml adaptation.
After Item 2.8 PASSES, proceed to Item 2.9 (single Stage-2 commit folding ALL working tree work + the 4 NEW V matrix tests + the docstring fix; pyproject.toml MUST be in commit per i1-3; commit template per v5 §work_remaining §2.9). Then Item 2.10 (append-only STATUS append with the 12-item cumulative footnote set).
Stop conditions during 2.8 / 2.9 / 2.10 (unchanged): - $BLARAI_PID drift at 2.8 → STOP and report. NO silent regex/yaml adaptation. - pytest baseline regression below 351 → STOP and investigate. The docstring fix is runtime-no-op so 351 should hold. - 2.9 commit producing more than ONE Stage-2 commit (or omitting pyproject.toml or any V matrix test or any 2.6/2.7.v2 work) → STOP and investigate. - 2.10 STATUS append failing to include any of the 12 cumulative footnote items → STOP and investigate. - Context exhaustion at any clean breakpoint (2.8-close-pre-commit) → emit v6 mid-stage handoff XML (docs-only commit, ONE file in diff). NO partial-refactor commits. NO emergency truncated 2.9 commits.
Standing invariants unchanged: single Stage-2 commit at item 2.9 only; fleet stays paused (NEVER state.resume_fleet -- AttributeError); no merge to main; stash@{0} untouched; STATUS append-only at item 2.10. Standing reminders r1-r9 + g6-ea6_n7 + g6-ea6_n8 + g6-ea6_n9 apply throughout.
After Item 2.10 lands, EA-6 emits the closing handoff to LA per the <closing_handoff_to_la_at_stage_2_close> block in the EA-6 init pointer (Stage 2 BlarAI single commit hash, V1-V7 outputs concise table, per-item V8 results, V matrix V4-V7 test counts + names, A11+ anomalies in STATUS.md, decision narrative on D2/D3/D4 + D2-completion + D1 + V4 + g6-ea6_n9 docstring fix, etc.).
